content.verdict: COMO Shambhala Estate is a 23-acre wellness sanctuary in the jungle above Begawan Giri outside Ubud, built around five residences designed by Cheong Yew Kuan. It is the island's serious wellness destination — Ayurveda, Ayurvedic cuisine, hydrotherapy, resident doctors — and a transformative choice for couples who want their honeymoon to actually reset something. Not a spa hotel with yoga tacked on; this is a retreat with hotel-grade luxury bolted on.
content.best_room: Book a one-bedroom suite within Tirta Ening, the residence that sits directly over the Ayung River — the suites face a private infinity pool with jungle canopy and river sounds below. Alternatively, the Wanakasa treehouse suites put you up in the jungle canopy among the ficus.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Wellness Consultation
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Denpasar (90 min). On arrival, meet with the resident Ayurvedic doctor or wellness consultant for a dosha assessment and a bespoke 6-day plan. Light plant-based dinner at Kudus House.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ayurveda Day 1 & Yoga
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn meditation, sunrise vinyasa in the open-air shala. Ayurvedic breakfast. Morning Ayurvedic treatments — abhyanga four-hand massage and shirodhara. Private forest walk with a naturalist. Dinner at Glow.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Hydrotherapy & Forest Bathing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning Watsu aquatic bodywork session as a couple. Shambhala hydrotherapy circuit — vitality pool, ice plunge, steam. Afternoon forest-bathing walk to the sacred spring. Candlelit dinner in your suite.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ubud Exploration Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Break from the program: chauffeured half-day into Ubud town. Ubud market, Puri Saren, Antonio Blanco museum, vegetarian lunch at Alchemy. Return for evening yoga nidra.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Personal Training & Couples Reset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Couples' fitness session with a personal trainer — guided jungle hike combined with functional training. Breathwork workshop together. Evening couples' sound bath followed by dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Temple & Purification Ceremony
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private visit to Tirta Empul for the melukat purification ritual with the estate's cultural guide. Ayurvedic lunch riverside. Farewell couples' spa ritual — flower bath, boreh scrub, two-therapist massage.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Integration & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final sunrise yoga session. Nutritionist consultation with a plan to take home. Leisurely Ayurvedic brunch, airport transfer. Most couples leave noticeably lighter than they arrived.

caveats: This is a wellness retreat — no burgers, minimal alcohol, and the kitchen leans plant-based and macrobiotic. If you wanted a pure indulgence honeymoon, pair it with a few nights at a beach resort.
The estate is built on a steep ravine; buggies run constantly but there are stairs everywhere.
It's not adults-only officially but families are very rare — expect a hushed, adult-serious atmosphere.

faqs.q: Do we have to do the wellness program?
faqs.a: No — COMO Shambhala can be enjoyed as a conventional luxury resort. But most guests commit to at least the basic consultation and a few treatments; it's the point of the property.

faqs.q: Is there a private pool in every suite?
faqs.a: The residence-level suites share infinity pools within each residence building (e.g. Tirta Ening's infinity pool is shared by the 3-4 suites inside). The private villas (Umabona, etc.) have fully private pools.
The COMO Shambhala Cuisine menu is primarily plant-based and raw-leaning, but Glow restaurant also serves fish, free-range poultry and moderate alcohol. You will not feel deprived.

faqs.q: Is this suitable for a first-time Bali visitor?
faqs.a: Yes, but pair with a beach stay — Shambhala will not show you the classic Bali beach/surf/sunset experience. Two nights at Amankila or Four Seasons Jimbaran plus 5 nights here is a popular combo.

faqs.q: What's the difference between Shambhala Estate and Como Uma Ubud?
faqs.a: Uma Ubud is the sister property closer to town — cheaper, more conventional, perfectly lovely. The Estate is the serious wellness flagship up the mountain, with higher staff ratios and clinical capabilities.

faqs.q: Can we book individual treatments without signing up for a program?
faqs.a: Absolutely — every treatment is a la carte. The programs just bundle them with consultations, meals and goals.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon enquiry — Tirta Ening suite, 7 nights, [DATES]

Dear COMO Shambhala reservations,

We are planning a wellness-focused honeymoon and would like 7 nights from [ARRIVAL] to [DEPARTURE], ideally in a one-bedroom suite in Tirta Ening (river view).

Could you please send us:
- All-inclusive wellness package options vs. nightly rate + treatments a la carte
- Availability of the Ayurvedic doctor on arrival day
- Any couples' bespoke programs
- Airport transfer options and cost
- Honeymoon inclusions (if any)

We're aiming to make this a meaningful reset as well as a celebration.

With thanks,
[Your names]
